items.proofTheme: Agent governance, protocol controls, and procurement-safe proof
items.operatingQuestion: Do we have one reusable AI proof pack that can answer board, buyer, and diligence questions without rework?
items.currentProofPack: The scorecard, protocol, and bridge estate already form a strong reusable AI proof packet.
items.headlineGap: Needs one tighter board memo that turns the technical proof into a one-page risk story.
items.boardStory: AI oversight gets easier to defend when the proof reads like one governed operating system instead of scattered demos.
items.nextMove: Package the AI scorecard, protocol suite, and bridge surfaces into one board-ready proof room.

items.proofTheme: Identity governance, remediation ownership, and endpoint control evidence
items.operatingQuestion: Can we prove role drift, remediation ownership, and app-protection posture as one reusable identity story?
items.currentProofPack: The access, protection, and cross-risk surfaces already support a repeatable identity proof packet.
items.headlineGap: Needs one cleaner reuse path that keeps ownership, drift, and remediation together.
items.boardStory: Identity proof creates trust when control ownership and remediation timing are visible in one place.
items.nextMove: Unify identity, endpoint, and remediation proof into one reusable evidence pack.

items.proofTheme: Reporting governance, experimentation, and attribution integrity
items.operatingQuestion: Can we prove revenue-system rigor without making leaders assemble the story from separate reporting and MarTech surfaces?
items.currentProofPack: The reporting and MarTech estate is credible, but the board-facing proof is still spread across too many separate repos.
items.headlineGap: Needs one revenue proof packet that packages reporting, attribution, and experiment controls into one narrative.
items.boardStory: Revenue proof gets stronger when experimentation and reporting controls read as one accountable system.
items.nextMove: Turn the reporting, Power BI, Tableau, and MarTech proofs into one revenue evidence room.

items.proofTheme: Payments, merchant risk, treasury, and KYC proof packaging
items.operatingQuestion: Can we show FinTech diligence reviewers a coherent packet for payments, KYC, treasury, and merchant risk?
items.currentProofPack: The core surfaces are there, but the investor-safe packet still needs sharper benchmarking and reuse discipline.
items.headlineGap: Needs stronger benchmark context and less manual legal interpretation for FinTech proof.
items.boardStory: FinTech trust capital increases when the proof room feels pre-assembled instead of bespoke for every investor question.
items.nextMove: Create one FinTech proof room spanning payments, merchant, treasury, and KYC.

items.proofTheme: GxP quality, CAPA, specimen handling, and safety review proof
items.operatingQuestion: Can we answer qualification reviewers with one conservative packet for deviations, custody, change control, and safety?
items.currentProofPack: The biotech stack is strong, but the qualification-safe proof packet still needs a more explicit assembly.
items.headlineGap: Needs a tighter qualification-ready room that keeps posture, caveats, and proof links together.
items.boardStory: Regulated buyers trust the system more when quality proof is coherent, conservative, and easy to audit.
items.nextMove: Package GxP, CAPA, specimen, narrative, and safety proofs into one quality evidence room.

items.proofTheme: RFP, disclosure, procurement evidence, and public-sector suitability
items.operatingQuestion: Can we provide a public-sector proof room that stays explicit about disclosure, response reuse, and procurement readiness?
items.currentProofPack: The ingredients exist, but the public-sector proof packet is still blocked by missing ownership and packaging.
items.headlineGap: Blocked by missing public-sector proof room ownership and benchmark framing.
items.boardStory: Public-sector expansion is still constrained by proof packaging discipline more than raw capability.
items.nextMove: Build the first public-sector proof room with disclosure, procurement, and answer-library artifacts.

items.proofTheme: Clinical disclosure, diagnostics, and patient-risk posture
items.operatingQuestion: Can we prove healthcare trust with a packet that is cautious, evidence-linked, and easy to audit?
items.currentProofPack: The healthcare and diagnostics estate already supports careful proof, and the packet is close to reusable now.
items.headlineGap: Needs one sharper board memo that turns diagnostics and disclosure into a calmer investor story.
items.boardStory: Healthcare trust becomes easier to defend when one proof room carries disclosure, diagnostics, and review posture together.
items.nextMove: Ship a clinical proof room tied to care, diagnostics, and disclosure surfaces.
